page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor. Running on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The CPU configuration features a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring dedicated physical execution resources without hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include any local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a compute-focused option. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable CPU execution, though the lack of hyperthreading and local storage represents a tradeoff that requires external storage integration. This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring consistent compute power rather than large memory footprints or local disk I/O.

Appropriate workloads for this configuration include compute-intensive tasks such as batch processing, web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and application servers that do not require local storage or high memory-to-CPU ratios.

The compute-heavy resource allocation makes this instance suitable for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, application hosting, and CI/CD pipelines where predictable CPU performance is prioritized over large memory capacities or local storage. This profile offers a balanced approach for compute-intensive tasks requiring dedicated processor access.
